summary: Write-time gating appears to improve distractor-heavy memory retrieval, while archive-plus-lineage preserves prior-state access, but the mechanism claims remain bounded to the paper's evaluated settings.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The source-label signal contributed only a modest gain on Wikipedia at 4:1 distractors.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence shows source reputation and reliability are part of scoring and that removing source labels reduced accuracy only modestly in one Wikipedia setting; this supports only the bounded claim that source labels were helpful but not the sole driver there, not a stronger claim about the full scoring function across domains.
